title: rasa.app.v1 — canvas app manifest (app.json)
description: The manifest every canvas app directory carries. Shape contract only — cross-field law (registry exhaustiveness, event coverage, nav targets) is audited by bin/check-app per content/APP_MODEL.md.

id.description: kebab-case, stable, never renamed after creation

version.description: the app's semver: patch = data/state refresh, minor = screen changed/added, major = app model reshaped

screens.description: exhaustive — every file in screens/ has a row here and vice versa

active_screen.description: the screen currently projected onto the canvas; must be a registered screen id

path.description: tenant-relative; never outside the tenant root

events.description: exhaustive — every action any published screen can emit has a row; EVENT executes the row exactly

emits.description: region id(s) that emit this action

handling.description: what the user was promised — the declared behavior

writes.description: what this action mutates, in write-order: bound-collection entries execute per the executor rule (PROCESSES.md §EVENT — module-declared procedure first, direct conventional writes only on writable collections), {state} entries are app-local memory

state.description: an app-local state/ file this action writes

bindings.description: the binding registry (APP_MODEL.md §bindings) — one row per bound region; ids unique; cross-field law (region/screen existence, context.json resolution, read-write-has-writer) audited by bin/check-app

screen.description: the screen that region lives on

module.description: dotted module name — must resolve in context.json#modules

collection.description: collection id (optionally id/sub-path) declared by that module

tenant.description: tenant-relative file path (read-only sugar equivalent: data_sources[])

reactive.description: absent = on-event (today's mechanism); live requires the kernel file-event bridge (KERNEL_ASKS #11)

provisioned.description: true when this binding's records were provisioned by this app (provision-then-bind)
